---
title: "Sleep Project"
author: "Sejal Patel"
date: "2024-04-27"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r}

library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(MASS)
library(glmnet)
library(olsrr) 

if (!requireNamespace("janitor", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("janitor")
library(janitor)

if (!requireNamespace("DataExplorer", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("DataExplorer")
library(DataExplorer)

if (!requireNamespace("car", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("car")
library(car)

Sleep_health_and_lifestyle_dataset <- read.csv("sleep.csv")

# ***************************************************
#                   CLEANING DATA                   *
# ***************************************************

# Getting rid of PersonID column
sleep <- Sleep_health_and_lifestyle_dataset[, -1]


# Renaming columns
colnames(sleep) <- c("Gender", "Age", "Occupation", "Sleep_Duration", "Sleep_Quality", "Physical_Activity", "Stress_Level", "BMI_Category", "BP", "Heart_Rate", "Daily_Steps", "Sleep_Disorder")

# Changing "Normal Weight" to "Normal" in BMI column
sleep$BMI_Category[which(sleep$BMI_Category == "Normal Weight")] <- "Normal"

# checking for NAs
sum(is.na(sleep))
is.na(sleep)


# ***************************************************
#                   DUMMY VARIABLES                 *
# ***************************************************

# Data preparation:

sleep %>% 
  clean_names() %>% 
  glimpse()

# Now you can use the introduce() function
introduce(sleep)


#checking for duplicates and na
sum(duplicated(sleep))
sum(is.na(sleep))

# separate() to split column blood_pressure
sleep <- separate(sleep, BP, into = c("systolic", "diastolic"), sep = "/")
sleep$systolic <- as.numeric(sleep$systolic)
sleep$diastolic <- as.numeric(sleep$diastolic)

# ************************************************
#                         MODEL                  *
# ************************************************

y <- sleep$Sleep_Duration
x1 <- sleep$Gender
x2 <- sleep$Age
x3 <- sleep$Occupation
x4 <- sleep$Sleep_Quality
x5 <- sleep$Physical_Activity
x6 <- sleep$Stress_Level
x7 <- sleep$BMI_Category
x81 <- sleep$systolic
x82 <- sleep$diastolic
x9 <- sleep$Heart_Rate
x10 <- sleep$Daily_Steps
x11 <- sleep$Sleep_Disorder

# turning categorical variables into factors so we can use in model
sleep <- sleep %>%
  mutate_at(vars(Gender, Occupation, BMI_Category, Sleep_Disorder), as.factor) %>%
  mutate_if(is.integer, as.numeric)
  

# sleep duration is response var, using rest of the vars as predictors
full_model <- lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Age + Sleep_Quality + Physical_Activity + Stress_Level + Heart_Rate + Daily_Steps + Gender + Occupation + BMI_Category + systolic + diastolic + Sleep_Disorder, data = sleep)
summary(full_model)


#deciding to remove certian vars
vif(full_model)
full_model <- lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Age + Sleep_Quality + Physical_Activity + Stress_Level + Heart_Rate + Daily_Steps + Gender + Occupation + BMI_Category + systolic + Sleep_Disorder, data = sleep)

# slide 22 Lecture 10
fit0 <- lm(Sleep_Duration~1, data = sleep)


#forward --- only removed 2 variables
reduced_model <- stepAIC(fit0, direction = "forward", scope=list(lower=fit0, upper=full_model))

reduced_model <- stepAIC(full_model, direction = "backward")

reduced_model <- stepAIC(fit0, direction = "both", scope=list(lower=fit0, upper=full_model))

summary(reduced_model)

# can run lasso for simpler model, different hyperparam.
# table on report
x=sleep[, c(1:3, 5:13)]
x = as.matrix(x)
y=sleep[,"Sleep_Duration"]
lambdas <- 10^seq(2, -3, by=-.1)



lasso_reg <- cv.glmnet(x, y, alpha=1, lambda=lambdas, standardize=TRUE, nfolds=3)
lasso_reg$lambda.min 

lasso_model <- glmnet(x, y, alpha=1, lambda=0.001, standardize=TRUE)

# . means its close to 0, choose the rest
# lasso can reduce coeff to zero unlike other ridge
lasso_model$beta
# compare with aic







# best subset selection LEcture notes page 17


### fit the linear model all subset selection 
ms_all=ols_step_all_possible(full_model) 

ms_all[which.max(ms_all$result$adjr)] 



result=ms_all$result
result[2036,]


#stepwise with p-values 
ols_step_forward_p(full_model) 

ols_step_backward_p(full_model)

# each model corresponds to the models from the stepAIC forward
# can compare anova with forward variable selection w table


anova(lm(Sleep_Duration ~ 1, data = sleep),
      lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Sleep_Quality, data = sleep),
      lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Sleep_Quality + systolic + diastolic, data = sleep),
      lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Sleep_Quality + systolic + diastolic + Occupation, data = sleep),
      lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Sleep_Quality + systolic + diastolic + Occupation + Physical_Activity, data = sleep),
      lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Sleep_Quality + systolic + diastolic + Occupation + Physical_Activity + Gender, data = sleep),
      lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Sleep_Quality + systolic + diastolic + Occupation + Physical_Activity + Gender + Daily_Steps, data = sleep),
      lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Sleep_Quality + systolic + diastolic + Occupation + Physical_Activity + Gender + Daily_Steps + Heart_Rate, data = sleep),
      lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Sleep_Quality + systolic + diastolic + Occupation + Physical_Activity + Gender + Daily_Steps + Heart_Rate + Stress_Level, data = sleep),
      lm(Sleep_Duration ~ Sleep_Quality + systolic + diastolic + Occupation + Physical_Activity + Gender + Daily_Steps + Heart_Rate + Stress_Level + Sleep_Disorder, data = sleep))

### not sure how to resolve the errors when line 110 is run
# looking for larger values --> variable is problematic
#make scatter plot of rleated vars
vif(reduced_model)







```
